npc_behavior_system.description: NPC behavior configuration for prehistoric survival game — defines AI routines, threat responses, and social dynamics for all non-player characters

TRex_BT.description: Apex predator — solitary hunter with large territory

PATROL.description: Moves slowly through territory, sniffing for prey

ALERT.description: Has detected movement or scent — scanning environment

ATTACK.description: Within strike range — lunging bite attack

FEEDING.description: Consuming a kill — highly aggressive if disturbed

sensory_profile.note: T-Rex has poor frontal vision but excellent smell and hearing

Raptor_BT.description: Pack hunter — coordinates with pack members, uses flanking tactics

PATROL.description: Pack moves together in loose formation, scanning for prey

STALK.description: Pack has spotted prey — moving to encircle before attacking

COORDINATED_ATTACK.description: Alpha signals — all members attack simultaneously from different angles

SCATTER.description: Pack member killed — temporary scatter before regrouping

Triceratops_BT.description: Herd animal — defensive, territorial around young, charges when threatened

GRAZE.description: Herd grazes peacefully — young in center, adults on perimeter

ALERT.description: Threat detected — herd stops, adults face threat

DEFENSIVE_FORMATION.description: Adults form wall between threat and young

RETREAT.description: Herd moves young to safety after repelling threat

Brachiosaurus_BT.description: Gentle giant — passive unless cornered or young threatened

BROWSE.description: Feeding on treetops — slow movement, long neck sweeping

MIGRATE.description: Moving between feeding grounds — follows established routes

STARTLED.description: Sudden noise or approach — rears up, stomps ground

FLEE.description: Persistent threat — moves away at speed

daily_routines.description: Time-of-day behavior modifiers for all dinosaur species

npc_memory_system.description: Persistent memory that shapes NPC behavior based on past interactions

threat_memory.player_attack: Increases aggression radius by 50% for 10 minutes
threat_memory.player_proximity: Logged if within 200 units — increases alertness on next encounter
threat_memory.player_killed_packmate: Pack permanently hostile to player in this zone

territory_memory.water_sources: All species remember water source locations
territory_memory.shelter_locations: Used during storms and extreme heat

social_memory.pack_bonds: Raptors remember pack hierarchy — alpha death causes temporary chaos
social_memory.maternal_bond: Mothers remember nest location and offspring for full season

raptor_flanking.text: The raptors circle before they strike. One draws your attention from the front while the others move to your flanks. Do not watch the decoy. Watch the shadows at the edge of your vision. That is where the real threat comes from.

maternal_aggression.text: A lone predator is dangerous. A hungry predator is desperate. But a mother protecting her nest — that is something else entirely. You have wandered too close. Leave. Now. Do not run. Back away slowly and do not break eye contact.

integration_notes.for_agent_12_combat_ai: Behavior tree states map directly to combat phases. ATTACK state triggers combat AI takeover. SCATTER state is recovery phase after combat loss.
integration_notes.for_agent_10_animation: Each state has a corresponding animation set. STALK uses crouch blend space. CHARGE uses root motion animation.
integration_notes.for_agent_16_audio: Vocalization intervals defined per state. Each state transition should trigger appropriate sound cue.
integration_notes.for_agent_13_crowd: Pack and herd behaviors use Mass AI formation system. Pack size ranges defined above.
integration_notes.ue5_implementation: Behavior Trees implemented via UE5 BehaviorTree asset system. Each BT node maps to a C++ BTTask or BTDecorator class in /Script/TranspersonalGame
